channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Prime Minister Carney Visits Armenia to Discuss Trade, Investment, and European Security Cooperation
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is scheduled to visit Yerevan, Armenia, from Saturday to Monday to participate in the European Political Community summit. The visit, which marks Canada as the first non-Eur...

item.title: The Role of Fashion in Courtroom Perceptions: Rebel Wilson and Other High-Profile Cases
item.description: Rebel Wilson, known for her vibrant on-screen persona, has adopted a restrained, court-appropriate wardrobe during her defamation trial against actor Charlotte MacInnes. Sporting white button-down shi...

item.title: Amaechi Urges Supporters to Avoid Social Media Fights Ahead of ADC Primary
item.description: Former Rivers State Governor and ADC chieftain, Rotimi Amaechi, don tok say him supporters no go dey engage for social media wahala as the party dey prepare for im presidential primary. Amaechi yarn d...

item.title: 66-year-old Man Charged After Car Bomb Explosion Outside Police Station in Northern Ireland
item.description: A 66-year-old man has been charged in connection with a car bomb attack outside a police station in Northern Ireland. The explosion occurred late at night and was reportedly intended to target police ...

item.title: Uncertainty Surrounds AI Investment and Semiconductor Market Trends
item.description: The recent surge in artificial intelligence (AI) has significantly boosted interest in semiconductor companies, despite the industry's historically volatile nature. The PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index...

item.title: AIS technology helps swimmer Olivia Wunsch refine starts and turns ahead of major competitions
item.description: Australian swimmer Olivia Wunsch, a young star of the women's 4x100m freestyle relay team, is leveraging high-tech analysis at the Australian Institute of Sport (AIS) in Canberra to enhance her perfor...

item.title: Minister Makonda Calls for Stronger Refereeing Standards and VAR Introduction
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzanian Minister for Information, Culture, Arts and Sports, Paul Makonda, has highlighted the need for urgent reforms to improve refereeing in local football. Speaking during World P...

item.title: Chadema Resumes Political Rallies and Launches Fundraising Campaign for Constitutional Reforms
item.description: Chadema, Tanzania's main opposition party, has resumed its public rallies and announced a fundraising initiative following a significant Central Committee meeting in Dar es Salaam. The two-day meeting...

item.title: DESI Completes 3D Mapping of 47 Million Galaxies and Quasars, Offering Insights Into Dark Energy
item.description: The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has successfully completed its five-year survey ahead of schedule, mapping over 47 million galaxies and quasars. This surpasses its original goal of 34 ...

item.title: House Committee Avoids Opening VP Sara Duterte’s Tax Records Amid Impeachment Hearings
item.description: During the pre-trial hearings on the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte, the House Committee on Justice voted against opening a box containing her tax records, despite a subpoe...

item.title: Tiger carcass discovered in Goa forest, poaching suspected
item.description: In a worrying development for wildlife conservation, an adult tiger was discovered dead on Saturday in the forests of Sacorda, Dharbandora region, Goa. Authorities found the carcass in a partially dec...

item.title: Court Rejects Anticipatory Bail of Nida Khan in TCS Nashik Harassment Case
item.description: In a significant development at Nashik, the court on Saturday denied anticipatory bail to Nida Khan, a key accused in a case involving alleged sexual harassment and forced religious conversion at Tata...

item.title: Emergency Teams Respond to Flash Floods in Nairobi, Moyale, and Other Areas
item.description: Heavy rains in Kenya have caused flash floods in multiple areas including Nairobi and Moyale, causing serious disruptions and prompting emergency responses. In Nairobi, motorists were stranded on Momb...

item.title: Salvadoran Man Indicted After April Shooting During ICE Operation in California
item.description: Carlos Iván Mendoza Hernández, a 36-year-old Salvadoran man, was shot by ICE agents during an April 7 immigration operation in Patterson, California. The incident resulted in Hernández being hospitali...

item.title: Rogers announces voluntary buyouts, BoC maintains rates, and Canada introduces first sovereign wealth fund
item.description: This week in Canadian business news, several significant developments occurred. Rogers Communications has announced voluntary departure packages for up to half its workforce, excluding Maple Leaf Spor...

item.title: EU criticizes Trump’s proposed tariff increases
item.description: The European Union has formally responded to U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement regarding planned increases in tariffs. EU officials have described the proposed tariffs as 'unacceptable' and a...

item.title: Alberta Elections agency responds to unauthorized posting of voter data by separatist group
item.description: In late March, journalist Jen Gerson informed Elections Alberta of a potential privacy breach affecting nearly 2.9 million Albertans, after learning that voter information had been posted online by th...

item.title: Amnesty International Calls for Investigation into Deaths of Fulani Detainees in Kwara Camp
item.description: Amnesty International don raise alarm say at least 150 Fulani people, majority na children, don die inside military-controlled camp for Kwara State. Dem investigation show say di detainees, wey total ...

item.title: 31-year-old woman partially paralysed due to nitrous oxide misuse
item.description: A 31-year-old woman has revealed the severe consequences of becoming addicted to nitrous oxide, commonly known as 'laughing gas' or 'nangs'. At the height of her dependency, she inhaled up to 2,400 ca...

item.title: Electricity reaches Binagunda, bringing relief to a remote tribal hamlet in Maharashtra
item.description: The article covers a report on Binagunda, a remote tribal hamlet in Maharashtra, where villagers are finally receiving electricity support after long-standing hardship. The piece highlights how access...
